title: A New Era: Péter Magyar Becomes Prime Minister of Hungary
subtitle: After 16 years of Viktor Orbán’s leadership, the country begins a major political change.

summary.en: Péter Magyar has been officially sworn in as the new prime minister of Hungary. His center-right Tisza party won a landslide victory in the recent parliamentary elections. This event marks the end of Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. Magyar has promised to fight corruption and improve Hungary's relationship with the European Union. Thousands of citizens gathered in Budapest to celebrate the start of this new political chapter.
summary.zh: 馬札爾（Péter Magyar）已正式宣誓就任匈牙利新任總理。他所屬的中間偏右政黨「提薩黨」（Tisza）在最近的國會大選中取得壓倒性勝利。此事件標誌著奧班（Viktor Orbán）長達十六年統治的結束。馬札爾承諾將打擊貪腐並改善匈牙利與歐盟的關係。數千名公民聚集在布達佩斯，慶祝這個新政治篇章的開始。

paragraphs.en: On Saturday, Péter Magyar was officially sworn in as the new prime minister of Hungary. This event marks a historic shift in the country, as it brings an official end to the 16-year rule of Viktor Orbán. The change follows a landslide victory by Magyar’s center-right Tisza party in the parliamentary elections held last month. According to The Guardian World, the result brought great excitement to Budapest and other parts of the country, as many citizens felt ready for a new direction.
paragraphs.zh: 週六，馬札爾（Péter Magyar）正式宣誓就任匈牙利新任總理。此事件標誌著該國歷史性的轉變，正式終結了奧班（Viktor Orbán）長達十六年的統治。在此之前，馬札爾所屬的中間偏右政黨「提薩黨」（Tisza）在上個月舉行的國會大選中取得壓倒性勝利。根據《衛報世界》（The Guardian World）報導，選舉結果為布達佩斯及該國其他地區帶來了巨大的興奮，因為許多公民都覺得是時候迎接新的方向了。

paragraphs.en: During the ceremony, Magyar spoke to thousands of supporters who gathered outside the parliament building. He described the path to this moment as long and difficult. He told the crowd that the Hungarian people had given his party a clear mandate—a formal instruction from voters—to change the government and the entire system. "A mandate not only to change the government, but to change the system as well. To start again," Magyar stated, as reported by Deutsche Welle. He promised to build a more inclusive country where everyone feels they have a place.
paragraphs.zh: 在就職典禮上，馬札爾向聚集在國會大廈外的數千名支持者發表了演說。他形容走到這一刻的道路漫長而艱辛。他告訴群眾，匈牙利人民已經賦予他的政黨明確的授權——來自選民的正式指示——來改變政府和整個體制。根據德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）報導，馬札爾表示：「這不僅是改變政府的授權，更是改變整個體制的授權。我們要重新開始。」他承諾將建立一個更具包容性的國家，讓每個人都覺得自己有一席之地。

paragraphs.en: Under the previous government, Hungary faced many challenges, including concerns about corruption and the rule of law. Magyar has pledged to address these issues directly. He stated that his government would seek justice against those who acted unfairly during the previous administration. He also called for officials appointed by the former government to resign, specifically mentioning the president, Tamás Sulyok. The crowd outside the parliament cheered loudly at these remarks, showing strong support for his plans to reform the nation.
paragraphs.zh: 在前任政府執政期間，匈牙利面臨許多挑戰，包括對貪腐和法治的擔憂。馬札爾已承諾將直接處理這些問題。他表示，他的政府將會對前任政府期間行為不公的人追究責任。他還呼籲前任政府任命的官員辭職，並特別點名了總統蘇尤克（Tamás Sulyok）。國會大廈外的群眾對這些言論報以熱烈的掌聲，顯示出對他改革國家計畫的強烈支持。

paragraphs.en: For many Hungarians, this day represented a moment of hope. Erzsébet Medve, a 68-year-old teacher who traveled for hours to attend the event, told The Guardian World that she finally felt it was good to be Hungarian. She explained that she had felt frustrated for years because the previous government did not provide enough money for the education system. Another attendee, 70-year-old Marianna Szűcs, expressed her hope that the country would become more livable for her children and grandchildren. As a symbolic gesture of the new government's goals, the European Union flag was raised on the parliament building for the first time since 2014.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多匈牙利人來說，這一天代表著希望的時刻。68歲的教師梅德韋（Erzsébet Medve）為了參加這場活動，長途跋涉數小時，她告訴《衛報世界》，她終於覺得身為匈牙利人是件好事。她解釋說，多年來她一直感到沮喪，因為前任政府沒有為教育系統提供足夠的資金。另一位70歲的參與者蘇奇（Marianna Szűcs）則表達了她的希望，期盼國家能為她的子孫變得更宜居。作為新政府目標的象徵性姿態，歐盟旗幟自2014年以來首次在國會大廈升起。

paragraphs.en: Magyar faces a difficult task in the coming months. According to Deutsche Welle, his party won 141 of the 199 seats in parliament. This gives him the two-thirds majority needed to change the constitution and pass major reforms. One of his most important goals is to repair ties with the European Union. Relations between Hungary and the EU were often tense under Orbán, who frequently disagreed with the bloc on key issues. Magyar hopes to unblock billions of euros in EU funding that were frozen due to concerns about corruption and the rule of law. Accessing this money is seen as a vital step to help the economy grow.
paragraphs.zh: 馬札爾在未來幾個月將面臨艱鉅的任務。根據德國之聲報導，他的政黨在國會199個席次中贏得了141席。這使他獲得了修改憲法和通過重大改革所需的三分之二多數。他最重要的目標之一是修復與歐盟的關係。在奧班執政期間，匈牙利與歐盟的關係經常緊張，奧班經常在關鍵議題上與歐盟意見不合。馬札爾希望解凍數十億歐元的歐盟資金，這些資金因對貪腐和法治的擔憂而被凍結。獲得這筆資金被視為幫助經濟成長的關鍵一步。

paragraphs.en: In addition to political reforms, the new prime minister must manage a challenging economic situation. The Hungarian economy only recently started to recover from a period of stagnation—a time when the economy does not grow. Now, it faces new pressure from rising energy costs linked to the war in the Middle East. Despite these obstacles, Magyar remains focused on his promise to create a more free and humane Hungary. As he told his supporters, he believes that what connects the people of Hungary is stronger than what divides them. With the swearing-in complete, the country now looks toward a future that many hope will be more open to the rest of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 除了政治改革，新任總理還必須應對充滿挑戰的經濟局勢。匈牙利經濟最近才剛從一段停滯期——經濟沒有成長的時期——中復甦。現在，它面臨著中東戰爭導致能源成本上漲帶來的新壓力。儘管面臨這些障礙，馬札爾仍專注於他承諾要建立一個更自由、更人道的匈牙利。正如他告訴支持者的那樣，他相信連結匈牙利人民的力量比分裂他們的力量更強大。隨著宣誓就職的完成，這個國家現在正邁向一個許多人希望會對世界更加開放的未來。

keyTerms.definitionEn: An overwhelming majority of votes for one side in an election.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（山崩），這裡用來形容選舉結果。文中：The change follows a landslide victory by Magyar’s center-right Tisza party in the parliamentary elections held last month.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Connections or relationships between people or groups.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（綁鞋帶），這裡作名詞指國家間的關係。文中：One of his most important goals is to repair ties with the European Union.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To allow something to move or be accessed again after it was stopped or restricted.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 解除封鎖、恢復（資金等）的使用。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於電腦或網路術語，這裡指恢復資金的撥款。文中：Magyar hopes to unblock billions of euros in EU funding that were frozen due to concerns about corruption and the rule of law.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Péter Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister ending 16-year Orbán era

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar sworn in as prime minister

quiz.question: What specific action did Péter Magyar propose regarding current government officials during his speech?

options.A: He asked all current members of parliament to step down immediately.
options.B: He requested that officials appointed by the previous administration, including the president, resign.
options.C: He demanded that the president be arrested for corruption.
options.D: He announced that he would personally appoint new officials to replace the entire cabinet.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Magyar 明確提到他呼籲由前政府任命的官員辭職，並特別點名了總統 Tamás Sulyok。選項 A 錯誤，因為他呼籲的是「前政府任命的官員」而非全體國會議員；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到的是尋求正義（seek justice）與辭職，並未提到立即逮捕；選項 D 誇大了他的行動，文中僅提到要求官員辭職，並未說明他將直接指派所有新官員。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why is the raising of the European Union flag on the parliament building considered a 'symbolic gesture'?

options.A: It indicates that Hungary has officially joined the European Union for the first time.
options.B: It shows that the new government intends to prioritize EU regulations over Hungarian law.
options.C: It reflects the new government's intent to reverse the previous administration's strained relationship with the EU.
options.D: It proves that the new government has already successfully secured the frozen EU funding.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段與第五段的推論，文章提到歐盟旗幟自 2014 年以來首次升起，而第五段指出 Orbán 時期與歐盟關係緊張，且資金因法治問題遭凍結。因此，升旗象徵新政府修復與歐盟關係的決心。選項 A 錯誤，匈牙利早已是歐盟成員；選項 B 過度解讀，文中並未提到放棄國家主權；選項 D 錯誤，這只是象徵性舉動，並不代表資金已經到位。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'stagnation' mean as it is used to describe the Hungarian economy?

options.A: A period of rapid and unstable financial growth.
options.B: A state of inactivity or lack of economic development.
options.C: A sudden decline in energy costs and trade.
options.D: A time of intense competition with neighboring countries.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章在第六段明確定義 stagnation 為「a time when the economy does not grow」（經濟不成長的時期）。在商業與經濟語境中，stagnation 指的是停滯不前。選項 A 與「成長」相反；選項 C 雖然提到經濟挑戰，但 stagnation 本身不等於能源成本下降；選項 D 與經濟停滯的定義無關。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the main message of the article?

options.A: Péter Magyar was elected primarily to resolve the country's energy crisis caused by the Middle East war.
options.B: The Hungarian people are divided over the new government's plans to change the constitution.
options.C: The election of Péter Magyar signifies a major political transition aimed at systemic reform and international reconciliation.
options.D: The previous government's 16-year rule was beneficial for the economy but failed to address social issues.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於報導 Péter Magyar 的就職，並強調這象徵匈牙利政治的重大轉變，包括對內進行系統性改革（如反貪腐、修憲），以及對外修復與歐盟的關係。選項 A 僅是提到的眾多挑戰之一，非核心目標；選項 B 與文中提到的「團結」訊息相左；選項 D 與文章對前政府的批評（如貪腐與法治問題）不符。
